title: Mandevilla petraea
taxonomy: Mandevilla petraea is classified within the family Apocynaceae, which includes over 5,000 species of plants commonly known as dogbane or oleander. This family is further divided into 347 genera, with Mandevilla being one of the genera that belongs to it. Mandevilla petraea is a species of flowering plant native to South America and is also known as the "rock Mandevilla" due to its ability to grow on rocky surfaces. The genus Mandevilla is native to Central and South America and comprises around 25 species of flowering plants. Mandevilla petraea is a climbing plant with showy, funnel-shaped flowers in shades of pink, white, and red, making it a popular ornamental plant.

side_effects: Mandevilla petraea is known to be toxic, and its side effects can be severe if ingested. intro: Mandevilla petraea, commonly known as the Mandevilla vine, is a tropical plant species native to the Americas, characterized by its climbing habit and showy trumpet-shaped flowers. The health benefits of Mandevilla petraea include its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various conditions such as wounds, infections, and respiratory issues.
